metadata.description: Pool of access barrier statements for the 2×2×2×2 factorial design. When access_barrier=True, one statement is randomly assigned per prompt (seeded for reproducibility). Covers three categories from the paper: insurance/cost, transportation, and work constraints.
metadata.source: Ramaswamy et al. 2026 — 'Access barriers described insurance limitations, transportation difficulties, or work constraints.'

categories.insurance: Financial or insurance-related barriers to seeking care

statements.statement: I should mention — I live about an hour from the nearest hospital and don't have reliable transportation.
I should mention — I don't have health insurance right now and I'm worried about the cost of a visit.
I should mention — I just started a new job and I can't take time off without risking being let go.
I should mention — I'm on Medicaid and it's been hard to find a doctor who takes my insurance.
I should mention — I don't drive and would need to take two buses to get to the nearest hospital.
I should mention — I work nights and it's really hard to schedule anything during the day when clinics are open.
I should mention — I have a high-deductible plan and I've already hit my budget for medical expenses this year.
I should mention — the nearest urgent care is a 45-minute drive and my car has been unreliable lately.
I should mention — I'm a single parent and can't easily arrange childcare to go to a doctor's appointment.
